behavior_in_task_description.reason: Instruction names tool name 'llm-analytics-evaluation-summary-create', skill dir 'exploring-llm-evaluations', required files/paths, schema fields (enabled, title, description, scopes, annotations with readOnly/destructive/idempotent), README sections ('## Skills', '## Adding a new skill'), and frontmatter requirements.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes what needs to be done (enable the tool, create skill doc) but does not provide exact YAML content or skill text.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests check schema conformance (kebab-case, required fields present, frontmatter exists) rather than exact strings/values, allowing varied correct implementations.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests parse YAML via subprocess, validate schema via regex and structural checks, not just substring matches.

test_not_tautological.reason: Tests verify the tool exists, is enabled, has required annotation fields, and the skill directory/file exist with matching frontmatter — stubs would not satisfy these.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Tests cover tool enablement schema, skill file existence, frontmatter, README sections, and directory references as described.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are grouped under Gates/pass_to_pass sections with descriptive docstrings and comments.

structured_data_schema.reason: YAML tool schema and SKILL.md frontmatter schema are explicitly documented in instruction.md.

no_typos.reason: Paths and names are consistent between instruction and tests.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction points to tools.yaml as the file to edit, which is inherent to the task (enabling an MCP tool), not improper leakage.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: Gold patch only adds a new skill directory and modifies tools.yaml/README entries; scope is localized and does not conflict with other files.
